calendar.matters:
CRC 3.1385 ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Apr-12-2019 Off Calendar

Motion For Attorney Fees, Costs And Expenses; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities
Off Calendar Mar-05-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Jacob Butler, Esq. 310-929-4900 of Strategic Legal Practices for Plaintiff through Court Call.
Micahel Sachs, Esq. 415-984-8530 of Clark Hill for Defendant FCA US LLC.

RULING - A R G U E D. The Court shares its views. Plaintiff argues discovery responses.
(as fully reported in the reporter's notes) The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

PLAINTIFF MIGUELITO BONIFACIO, SEZZE BONIFACIO's Motion For Attorney Fees, Costs And Expenses.

The Court adopts the tentative ruling. Plaintiffs Miguelito Bonifacio and Sezze Bonifacio's motion for attorney fees, costs, and expenses is granted in the amount of $17,714.50 in fees and $3,143.96 in costs and expenses. Plaintiffs' counsel unnecessarily and unreasonably expended 16.1 hours in preparing responses to written discovery after the parties had reached substantial agreement on the terms of their settlement, and 11.3 hours traveling to San Francisco and appearing at a January 15, 2019 hearing on an order to show cause that could have been avoided, had Plaintiffs dismissed the action within 45 days after they filed a Notice of Settlement of Entire Case on July 18, 2018, in which Plaintiffs represented that a request for dismissal would be filed no later than November 15, 2018. After subtracting those hours, the Court finds that a reasonable lodestar for counsel's work on the case is $17,714.50. The Court declines to award a multiplier. (See Hall v. FCA US LLC (E.D. Cal. May 21, 2018) 2018 WL 2298431 at *8 [declining to award multiplier in Song-Beverly Act case where, among other things, case did not present novel or difficult questions of law or fact, there was no evidence that the nature of the litigation precluded other employment by the attorneys, and the contingent nature of the fee award was outweighed by the other factors].)(as fully reported in the reporter's transcript)

Counsel for defendants to submit a proposed order that is verbatim with the Court's ruling in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/EPS)
